rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MONTOGMERY VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. And Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.S Motion To Abate
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAY 5, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendants Kaiser Gypsum Co. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.¿s motion to abate is denied as moot given the tentative ruling on plaintiff¿s motion to amend. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MONTOGMERY VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Second Motion To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAY 5, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to amend complaint to name Deborah Montgomery McIntyre as a plaintiff is granted. No opposition filed. The parties are directed to meet and confer in an effort to agree on a new trial date. If the parties so agree, they should submit a stipulated order. If they cannot agree, plaintiffs should file a motion in department 220 to be heard on a Tuesday, Wednesday or Thursday at 9:30am requesting that trial date be set. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD HOFF et al VS. BONDEX INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAY 5, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Defendant Crane co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Hoff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD HOFF et al VS. BONDEX INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAY 5, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Defendant Union Carbide Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 3 (Georgia-Pacific) and granted as to issues 1-2 and 4 -6 (Durabond, Kelly Moore Paco, breach of warranty, false representation and intentional tort). The depositions of Mr. Milner and Mr. Lehnert create a triable issue whether Mr. Hoff was exposed to Georgia-Pacific joint compound that contained asbestos attributable to defendant. As to the remaining issues, defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present admissible evidence creating a triable issue. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD HOFF et al VS. BONDEX INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAY 5, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Defendant Air & Liquid Systems Corp., successor by merger to Buffalo Pumps, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Hoff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. = (220/HEK)
